name: Emerald
description: Lightweight, portable 2D engine with built-in physics and audio

kind.comment: Code-first 2D game library with a render+input+audio+physics loop and no editor — classified as `framework` per RESEARCH.md, even though it uses an ECS (hecs) internally.

license.comment: MIT per GitHub license metadata (SPDX MIT) and README.

documentation.comment: docs.rs is the primary API reference (only ~10% of the crate is documented). No separate book or docs site; README covers usage.

first-release.comment: crates.io crate created Apr 4, 2020; GitHub repo created the same day.

latest-stable-version.comment: 0.3.214 is the max stable (and newest) version on crates.io, published Jun 4, 2023. No release since.

maintenance-status.comment: GitHub repository is archived (read-only) as of ~Nov 2024; last push Nov 24, 2024 and last crates.io release Jun 2023. No longer maintained.

maturity.comment: Pre-1.0 (0.3.x) with a rapidly churning patch version scheme, no widely documented shipped titles, and now archived. Scored experimental per the maturity-vs-stars guideline.

backing.comment: Solo hobby project by a single developer (Bombfuse); no company or foundation backing. Now archived.

implementation.comment: Pure Rust; latest versions render via wgpu with winit windowing, physics via rapier2d, ECS via hecs — no non-Rust engine wrapped. (Earlier versions rendered via miniquad.)

architecture.comment: Entity-Component-System model built on hecs; entities live in an EmeraldWorld and are iterated ECS-style. Games are driven by a code-first update/draw loop (no scene graph).

built-in-ecs.comment: Ships hecs as the primary way to structure game entities via the EmeraldWorld — ECS is the default data model, not an optional add-on.

dimensionality.comment: 2D-only engine ("A 2D rust game engine focused on portability"); no first-party 3D tooling.

scripting.comment: Gameplay is written in Rust; no embedded scripting language.

data-driven-scenes.comment: Entities/worlds are built in code; no first-party serialized scene/prefab format documented. Assets (sprites, aseprite, sounds, fonts) are loaded by path, but scenes are not authored to data files.

reflection-serialization.comment: No first-party runtime reflection or engine-provided game-state (de)serialization documented; hecs itself provides no reflection. A user could add serde manually.

hot-reload.comment: No code or asset hot-reloading documented. The portability design packs assets into the binary for WASM, which runs counter to a watch/reload workflow.

visual-editor.comment: Code-first framework with no graphical editor.

live-inspector.comment: No runtime world/entity inspector, first-party or de-facto.

asset-pipeline.comment: AssetStore loads and caches common formats (sprites/textures, aseprite, sounds, fonts) by path, and can pack assets into the binary for WASM. Better than raw include_bytes! (1) but synchronous with no processing/reprocessing pipeline or hot reload (3+). Scored 2.

cli-project-tooling.comment: No dedicated CLI, cargo plugin, or official starter templates — plain cargo project.

debug-profiling-tools.comment: No profiler integration (no Tracy/egui overlay/diagnostics subsystem) documented.

ide-support.comment: Nothing beyond standard Rust tooling (rust-analyzer just works); no engine-specific IDE integration.

rendering-backend.comment: Latest stable (0.3.214) renders via wgpu (^0.14) with winit (^0.27) for windowing. Earlier versions of Emerald rendered via miniquad; the tag reflects the current/last-released backend.

custom-shaders.comment: No documented first-party API for authoring/slotting custom shaders into the pipeline. A render_to_texture example exists but user-authored shaders are not surfaced as a feature.

sprites-tilemaps.comment: First-party sprite/texture loading and rendering, including aseprite files and sheets. Built-in tilemap support is not clearly documented; scored true on the strength of first-party sprites.

text-rendering.comment: Built-in font/label rendering via the fontdue dependency.

physics.comment: Physics ships in the core engine (rigid bodies + colliders exposed on the world), built on rapier2d under the hood.

audio.comment: In-core audio mixer with looping/playback exposed via the engine API; no separate bring-your-own audio crate required.

input.comment: First-party keyboard/mouse (Button enum) and gamepad (re-exports the `gamepad` crate) input. Touch may be reachable through the web/mobile layer but is not clearly documented as first-party; omitted.

animation.comment: Sprite-frame animation via built-in aseprite support (load an aseprite file and play named animations). No skeletal/state-machine animation.

ui-system.comment: No first-party in-game UI system documented; bring your own (e.g. egui).

networking.comment: No built-in or first-party networking/multiplayer support documented.

windows.comment: Listed under supported platforms in the README.

macos.comment: Listed under supported platforms in the README.

linux.comment: Listed under supported platforms in the README (also lists Raspberry Pi).

web-wasm.comment: Conflicting signals: portability to WASM is Emerald's stated design goal (assets are packed into the WASM binary to avoid network requests), and early miniquad-based versions reportedly ran on the web — but the README lists HTML5 under a 'work in progress / probably broken' section, and the latest wgpu+winit-based releases don't document a verified browser path. Could not confirm it actually runs in a browser, so null per the conflict guideline.

ios.comment: iOS is not listed as a supported or in-progress target in the README.

android.comment: README lists Android only under a 'work in progress / probably broken' section, not as officially supported/tested; scored false per the platform-verification guideline.

clean-build-time.comment: Not measured on a consistent reference machine this pass; prefer null over a guess per RESEARCH.md.

incremental-build-time.comment: Not measured this pass; prefer null over a guess.

binary-size.comment: Not measured this pass; prefer null over a guess.

web-bundle-size.comment: Web support could not be verified (see Web (WASM)) and no bundle was measured; null rather than a guess or N/A.

runtime-overhead.comment: Not measured with a defined methodology; prefer null over a guess.

github-stars.comment: Snapshot from the GitHub API on 2026-07-06 (repository archived).

crates-io-downloads.comment: All-time downloads of the emerald crate as of 2026-07-06.

last-release.comment: 0.3.214 published Jun 4, 2023 on crates.io — the most recent release.

shipped-titles.comment: No notable released commercial games widely documented as built with Emerald; usage appears to be hobby/jam projects.

community-size.comment: Single-maintainer hobby project, now archived; small handful of contributors and no active community hub.

plugin-asset-ecosystem.comment: No third-party plugin/asset ecosystem or registry around Emerald.

learning-resources.comment: README plus a set of examples in the repo; docs.rs is sparse (~10% documented) and there is no official book/tutorial/video ecosystem. Scored 2.
